key_insight: The paper integrates differentiable finite element support-structure modeling into stellarator coil optimization, enabling simultaneous improvement of magnetic performance and mechanical stress.
engineering_relevance: This makes stellarator coil design less trial-and-error by co-optimizing coil shape and clamp placement for better field accuracy, lower stress, and more practical manufacturability.
method: Open-source tool "coil-fem" couples stellarator coil optimization with differentiable structural mechanics.
Support structure effects are included directly in the optimization loop via differentiable FEA.
A penalty term is used to balance field error against RMS von Mises stress in the coil body.
evidence_signal: In the reported demonstration, the optimized coil set achieved 2.4× lower RMS von Mises stress while maintaining similar magnetic field error to the unoptimized baseline.
watch_item: Results are shown for a specific optimization setup, so performance may depend on geometry, material assumptions, and how accurately the differentiable FEA captures real support-contact behavior.
paper_title: Towards joint optimization of stellarator coils and support structures
